relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: YHWH speaks Passover again in the wilderness.
Liberation is remembered at its appointed time.
The feast is kept according to its full pattern.
Moses calls Israel into remembrance.
The people keep Passover in Sinai.
Those made ritually unclean through contact with death cannot participate that day.
They ask why they should lose their place in the offering.
Moses pauses to seek guidance from YHWH.
YHWH answers their concern.
Mourner and distant traveler receive another appointed time.
The second-month Passover carries the same bread and bitter herbs.
The remembered meal keeps its integrity.
Deliberate refusal differs from inability.
Sojourner and native share the same sacred doorway.
Cloud covers the dwelling-place; fire appears by night.
Presence remains visible through day and darkness.
When the cloud lifts, the people move; where it rests, they rest.
Journey and camp both answer the Divine command.
Long waiting is also faithful movement with YHWH.
Sometimes the pause is brief.
Sometimes guidance changes overnight.
Days, months, or longer—Presence sets the pace.
Israel learns to move and remain by YHWH's command.

contemplative_note: Numbers 9 holds accommodation and guidance together. The people speak, Moses listens, YHWH creates a second Passover, and the cloud teaches that faithful life includes both motion and unhurried waiting.
